Process Decisions chevron_right DEC-0002
Accepted Last updated: Oct 24, 2023

DEC-0002 — Lifecycle Phases and Human Gates

The Nexus SDLC requires a standardized progression model that allows autonomous agents to operate within predefined boundaries while ensuring strict human oversight at critical juncture points.

Decision

We adopt a linear-gated progression model. Every agent-led cycle must traverse four distinct phases. Progression from one phase to the next is restricted by automated validation and, in key cases, mandatory human approval.

Phase Description
Inception Problem definition and constraint mapping. Agent generates initial proposal.
Elaboration Technical specification and prototype scaffolding. Internal validation triggers.
Construction Core logic implementation and unit testing. Continuous integration active.
Transition Final hardening and deployment preparation. Requires final sign-off.

lock_open Human Gates — Non-Negotiable

The transition from **Elaboration** to **Construction** requires a manual cryptographic signature from a verified Human-in-the-Loop (HITL).

  • Cost estimation validation
  • Security architecture review
  • Intent alignment verification

Reasoning

Fully autonomous systems pose a risk of "drift" where agent goals diverge from initial human intent. By implementing fixed gates, we create a heartbeat mechanism that forces synchronization between technical execution and human strategy.
